children.label: ii
children.text: multiple signs of distress, which may include high rates of poverty, childhood obesity, academic failure, and juvenile delinquency, adjudication, or incarceration; and

children.label: iii
children.text: schools implementing comprehensive support and improvement activities or targeted support and improvement activities under .section 6311(d) of this title

children.heading: Full-service community schools
children.label: B Full-service community schools
children.text: The provision of assistance to public elementary schools or secondary schools to function as full-service community schools.

children.heading: Sufficient size and scope
children.label: 2 Sufficient size and scope
children.text: Each grant awarded under this subpart shall be of sufficient size and scope to allow the eligible entity to carry out the applicable purposes of this subpart.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: b Duration
children.text: A grant awarded under this subpart shall be for a period of not more than 5 years, and may be extended for an additional period of not more than 2 years.
with respect to a grant for activities described in , the performance metrics described in ; andsection 7274 of this titlesection 7274(h) of this title
with respect to a grant for activities described in , annual performance objectives and outcomes under .section 7275 of this titlesection 7275(a)(4)(C) of this title

children.heading: Matching funds
children.label: A Matching funds
children.text: Each eligible entity receiving a grant under this subpart for activities described in shall contribute matching funds in an amount equal to not less than 100 percent of the amount of the grant. Such matching funds shall come from Federal, State, local, and private sources.section 7274 of this title

children.heading: Private sources
children.label: B Private sources
children.text: The Secretary shall require that a portion of the matching funds come from private sources, which may include in-kind contributions.

children.heading: Adjustment
children.label: C Adjustment
children.text: The Secretary may adjust the matching funds requirement under this paragraph for applicants that demonstrate high need, including applicants from rural areas and applicants that wish to provide services on tribal lands.

children.heading: Financial hardship waiver
children.label: D Financial hardship waiver
children.text: The Secretary may waive or reduce, on a case-by-case basis, the matching requirement under this paragraph, including the requirement for funds from private sources, for a period of 1 year at a time, if the eligible entity demonstrates significant financial hardship.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each eligible entity receiving a grant under this subpart for activities described in shall provide matching funds from non-Federal sources, which may be provided in part with in-kind contributions.section 7275 of this title

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: B Special rule
children.text: The Bureau of Indian Education may meet the requirement of subparagraph (A) using funds from other Federal sources.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may not require any eligible entity receiving a grant under this subpart to provide matching funds in an amount that exceeds the amount of the grant award.

children.heading: Consideration
children.label: B Consideration
children.text: Notwithstanding this subsection, the Secretary shall not consider the ability of an eligible entity to match funds when determining which applicants will receive grants under this subpart.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: From the amounts allocated under subsection (a) for grants to eligible entities, the Secretary shall use not less than 15 percent of such amounts to award grants to eligible entities that propose to carry out the activities described in such subsection in rural areas.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: The Secretary shall reduce the amount described in paragraph (1) if the Secretary does not receive a sufficient number of applications of sufficient quality.

children.heading: Minimum number of grants
children.label: f Minimum number of grants
children.text: For each fiscal year, the Secretary shall award under this subpart not fewer than 3 grants for activities described in and not fewer than 10 grants for activities described in , subject to the availability of appropriations, the requirements of subsection (a)(2), and the number and quality of applications.section 7274 of this titlesection 7275 of this title
